input_candidates.truth_value: The nominal composition Fe-30%Mn-9%Al-0.9%C is nearly 15% less dense than quenched and tempered SAE 4130 steels.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Figure 2a (scatter plot embedded in text) gives 530-C age-hardening hardness values for low-P Fe-30Mn-9Al-XSi-0.9C steels: at 1 h, 0.59% Si = 210 BHN, 1.07% Si = 220 BHN, 1.56% Si = 215 BHN; at 10 h, 260, 280, 295 BHN; at 100 h, 340, 350, 345 BHN.

input_candidates.truth_value: Silicon additions decrease the melting point by 30°C/wt.% Si and increase fluidity for complex shape casting.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Figure 2b (scatter plot embedded in text) gives 530-C age-hardening hardness values for 1% Si steels with varying P: at 1 h, 0.001% P = 215 BHN, 0.006% P = 225 BHN, 0.018% P = 275 BHN, 0.043% P = 245 BHN; at 10 h, 260, 275, 360, 340 BHN; at 100 h, 325, 345, 380, 370 BHN.

input_candidates.truth_value: Silicon has been reported to suppress formation of brittle β-Mn during extended age hardening.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Table II contains additional tensile data for non-calcium-treated steels with higher P (0.06% P): 1.0Si ST UTS 687 MPa EL 44%; 1.4Si ST UTS 766 MPa EL 36%; 1.0Si aged 530-C 10h UTS 940 MPa EL 18%; 1.4Si aged 10h UTS 995 MPa EL 14%; 1.0Si aged 30h UTS 1065 MPa EL 8%; 1.4Si aged 30h UTS 1085 MPa EL 5%. A calcium-treated 0.78Si 0.007P as-cast alloy had YS 616 MPa, UTS 698 MPa, EL 17%.

input_candidates.truth_value: Hot-rolled Fe-30Mn-9Al-2.7Si-0.9C (0.023% P) as-hot-rolled CVN energy was only 23 J (L–T) and 22 J (T–L), transitioning to 160 J after 1100°C solution treatment and water quench.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Figure 7 scatter data indicate wrought Fe-30Mn-9Al-1.01Si-0.9C-0.5Mo UTS values of approximately 950 MPa (as-solution treated), 1050 MPa (2h aged), 1070 MPa (10h), and 1160 MPa (50h), with corresponding cast values of 760 MPa (20h) and 1000 MPa (50h). Wrought elongation ranged from about 15% at 1160 MPa UTS to 55% at 950 MPa UTS, while cast elongation was about 18% at 1000 MPa and 36% at 770 MPa.

input_candidates.truth_value: The mechanism of brittle cleavage-like fracture with aging is slipping off / glide plane decohesion along {111} slip planes, facilitated by planar slip and shear strain localization from κ-carbide precipitation.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Figure 11 line/scatter data for CVN versus temperature: 1.07% Si 12h aged at 530-C gives energies from 42 J at -40-C to 92 J at 25-C; 1.56% Si 12h aged gives 21 J at -40-C to 68 J at 25-C; 1.07% Si 30h aged gives 20 J at -40-C to 58 J at 25-C; 1.56% Si 30h aged gives 12 J at -40-C to 33 J at 25-C.

input_candidates.truth_value: Figure 1a: as-cast Fe-28Mn-3Al-0.9Si-1.2C-0.6Mo steel microstructure of large primary austenite dendrites. Figure 1b: hot-rolled Fe-29Mn-6Al-0.9C-0.29Si-0.44Nb-0.44Mo equiaxed austenite grains, average grain size 50 μm, pinned by NbC on grain boundaries.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Hot-rolled Fe-30Mn-9Al-2.7Si-0.9C with 0.023% P had CVN breaking energy of only 23 J (L-T) and 22 J (T-L) in the as-hot-rolled condition, improving to 160 J (L-T) after solution treatment for 10 min at 1100-C and water quenching.

input_candidates.truth_value: Figure 5: APT carbon concentration maps (2–6 at.% C) showing progress of spinodal decomposition in 0.006% P and 0.043% P steels aged 10 h at 530°C. Scale is in nanometers.
input_candidates.prediction_value: The density reduction of these lightweight steels is up to 15% compared with conventional quenched and tempered steels and 17% reduction potential is mentioned for Al contents between 4 and 12 weight percent.

input_candidates.truth_value: A key conclusion is that further improvement requires grain-refining techniques or cast preforms for subsequent forging. Development of dispersoids to interfere with planar slip from SRO or κ-carbide shearing is needed to improve notch toughness and fatigue.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Inclusion analysis (Fig. 15) shows total inclusion density similar between low-P Si-containing steels (62-78 inclusions/mm2), with the major difference being porosity: 0.59% Si steel had more than twice the porosity of 1.07% Si steel and almost double that of 1.56% Si steel.

input_candidates.truth_value: Misch metal (Ce and La) treatment combined with Ca and Ar stirring reduced phosphorus embrittlement effects and improved CVN impact energy from 33 J to 108 J in aged Fe-30Mn-9Al-1Si-0.9C-0.3Mo.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Solution-treated cast steels with low phosphorus exhibit room-temperature Charpy V-notch impact energies up to 200 J and dynamic fracture toughness over 700 kJ/m2.
